title: Third-Party Administered Benefits
modulePurpose:
Everyday Healthcare, Preventive Services, RX Benefits, and How Networks Work

COPY-PASTE TRAINING SECTION DOCUMENT
Module 2 — Third-Party Administered Benefits

Section Purpose
This section teaches agents how the third-party administered (TPA) benefits portion of the MightyWELL bundle works.
Every MightyWELL bundle is built using two major components:
Third-Party Administered Benefits — for everyday healthcare
Health Share Participation — for major medical events
This module focuses on the first component.
Agents should leave this section understanding:
what third-party administered benefits are
how everyday healthcare works
how preventive services work
how prescription benefits work
how in-network and out-of-network visits work
how reimbursement works
how to explain this clearly and confidently to customers
This is one of the most important sales sections in the entire training.
Most customer questions will come from this part of the plan.

moduleSteps.title: Step 1 — Review Overview
moduleSteps.subtitle: Review Overview
moduleSteps.copy:
The Two Parts of the Bundle
Every MightyWELL bundle is built using two core pieces.
Part 1 — Third-Party Administered Benefits
 Handles everyday healthcare.
Part 2 — Health Share Participation
 Supports major medical needs.
This module focuses on Part 1.

What Are Third-Party Administered Benefits?
Third-party administered benefits are healthcare services that are managed and coordinated by an independent administrator.
These services typically include:
routine doctor visits
urgent care visits
specialist visits
preventive services
prescription support
everyday healthcare needs
These benefits are not insurance.
They are structured healthcare services administered by a third-party organization.
This part of the bundle is designed to make routine healthcare:
Simple
 Predictable
 Affordable
 Easy to explain

The Big Idea Agents Must Understand
This is the everyday healthcare portion of the bundle.
This is what members use most often.
This is what customers ask about first.
And this is what you will explain in nearly every sales conversation.

You Can Go to Any Doctor You Want
This is one of the most important concepts to communicate clearly.
Members are free to visit any licensed healthcare provider they choose.
There are no restrictions on where members can receive care.
To make healthcare easier and more convenient, the program provides access to large national networks.
These include:
PHCS / MultiPlan
 First Health
These networks include hundreds of thousands of providers nationwide.
But remember this:
Networks are there for convenience, not limitations.

How In-Network Visits Work
If the provider is in network, the process is simple.
Step 1
 Show the member ID card.
Step 2
 Pay the applicable copay.
Step 3
 Receive care.
That’s it.
Most in-network visits follow this exact process.

How Out-of-Network Visits Work
If the provider is not in network, the member can still receive care.
There is no penalty for choosing an out-of-network provider.
Here’s how the process works.
Step 1
 Receive care.
Step 2
 Pay for the visit.
Most routine visits typically cost between:
$200 to $400
Examples include:
Primary care visits
 Urgent care visits
 Specialist visits
Step 3
 Upload the invoice in the MightyWELL app.
The member submits:
The invoice
 The date of service
 The reason for the visit
Step 4
 Receive reimbursement.
Reimbursement is typically issued within:
Approximately five business days
The reimbursement amount is:
Covered amount minus the applicable copay.

Preventive Services
Preventive services are designed to help members stay healthy and detect health issues early.
These services focus on maintaining health rather than treating illness.
Examples may include:
Annual wellness visits
 Routine screenings
 Preventive health evaluations
Preventive services support long-term health and help reduce the risk of more serious medical problems.
Agents should always refer to the official bundle guide for the exact preventive services included in each plan.

Prescription (RX) Benefits
Many MightyWELL bundles include prescription support.
Prescription benefits help members reduce the cost of medications.
Medication pricing may vary depending on:
The medication
 The pharmacy
 The specific plan
Agents should always reference the official bundle guide for:
Medication pricing
 Pharmacy options
 Coverage details

Sales Communication — Simple One-Liners
Agents should be able to explain routine care quickly and clearly.
Use language like this:
You can go to any doctor you want.
If they are in network, you show your card, pay your copay, and receive care.
If they are out of network, you pay for the visit, upload your invoice in the app, and receive reimbursement less your copay.

Another simple explanation:
In network is convenience.
 Out of network is flexibility.
 Either way, you can get care.

Another:
Networks make healthcare easier.
 They do not limit your options.

Another:
This part of the plan handles everyday healthcare — not major medical events.

moduleSteps.title: Step 2 — Instructions
moduleSteps.subtitle: Review Materials
moduleSteps.copy:
Before taking the quiz, complete the following:
Review the third-party administered benefits overview above
Open the bundle guide in the portal
Locate the following information:
Primary care visit copay
 Urgent care visit copay
 Specialist visit copay
 Preventive services
 Prescription benefit information
Keep the bundle guide open while taking the quiz
This training is open book.
Agents are expected to use official materials when answering questions.

moduleSteps.title: Step 3 — Quiz
moduleSteps.subtitle: Take Quiz
moduleSteps.copy:
(Open Book — 9 Questions)

Quiz Questions — Module 2
True or False
 Third-party administered benefits handle everyday healthcare needs.
Correct answer:
 True

Which of the following is part of the third-party administered benefits portion of the bundle?
A. Doctor visits
 B. Preventive services
 C. Prescription support
 D. All of the above
Correct answer:
 D

True or False
 Members are restricted to using only in-network providers.
Correct answer:
 False

What is the primary purpose of provider networks?
A. To limit where members can receive care
 B. To make healthcare more convenient
 C. To deny services outside the network
Correct answer:
 B

True or False
 Networks at MightyWELL are there for convenience, not limitations.
Correct answer:
 True

What happens when a member visits an out-of-network provider?
A. The visit is denied
 B. The member pays for the visit and submits the invoice through the app
 C. The provider must join the network
Correct answer:
 B

Approximately how long does reimbursement typically take after submitting the invoice?
A. Same day
 B. About five business days
 C. Thirty days
Correct answer:
 B

Which information must be submitted when requesting reimbursement?
A. The invoice
 B. The date of service
 C. The reason for the visit
 D. All of the above
Correct answer:
 D

True or False
 Third-party administered benefits are responsible for everyday healthcare, while the health share supports major medical needs.
Correct answer:
 True
